rulings.Case Title: FRANK BORN VS. MARIA GONZALEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Production Of Records From San Francisco Federal Credit Union And Vibrant Care Rehabilitation
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 15, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANTs MARIA GONZALEZ and LILA GONZALEZ-GARFINKEL's Motion To Quash Production Of Records From San Francisco Federal Credit Union And Vibrant Care Rehabilitation GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff's subpoenas are limited as follows: (1) Vibrant Care Rehabilitation - records from October 2011 to June 2013 reflecting dates of visits and dates treatments; the nature of medical condition and treatment shall be redacted; (2) San Francisco Federal Credit Union - monthly statements from October 2011 to June 2013; all transactions made by Maria Gonzalez shall be redacted and all amounts of transactions shall be redacted. Plaintiff shall bear the cost of redacting the documents. = (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN GERAGHTY VS. JOSEPH SHALIZI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 15, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT JOSEPH SHALIZI's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Moving party shifted the burden. Opposing party failed to present admissible evidence creating triable issue of fact. Specifically, no facts are presented to show that defendant misrepresented his intent to remove plaintiff from the premises by way of either buy-out agreement of Owner Move-In Eviction. Owner Move-In eviction has never been initiated due to plaintiff's acceptance of the buy-out agreement. No evidence is provided to show that plaintiff did not contemplate a possibility of the Owner Move-In Eviction at the time the representation was made. Whether defendant would have followed through with the eviction and whether plaintiff would have complied with the Rent Ordinance is speculative at best and cannot create a triable issue of fact. = (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PORTILLO, TRUSTEE OF THE ALFONSO PORTILLO VS. TINA SUY TROUNG et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 15, 2014, Line 8: DEFENDANT CLAUDIA ROJAS' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT: GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to allege either entitlement to rent or to daily damages; both are not recoverable for the same period and it is unclear what period rent is being requested for. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B in future. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIANO BUSTO et al VS. ALEJANDRO V. DIESTA JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 15, 2014, Line 9: DEFENDANT ALEX DIESTA, JR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash: DENIED. Alex Diesta is not a party to this action. No authority is cited for proposition that alleged failure to comply with CCP 474 is grounds for motion to quash by a non-party served with Pre-Judgment Claim of Right to Possession. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIANO BUSTO et al VS. ALEJANDRO V. DIESTA JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 15, 2014, Line 9: DEFENDANT ROSEALEX DIESTA's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS: DENIED, 5 days to respond. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that failure to name Alex Jr. as a named party deprives the Court of jurisdiction over a named party. (501/LKS)
